title: libSQL VisibilityStore implementation
purpose: Implement the VisibilityStore trait for the libSQL backend. Standard fields (workflow_type, status, start_time, close_time) are indexed columns for efficient filtering. Custom search attributes are stored as a typed JSON column. This enables the embedded deployment to support visibility queries without external search infrastructure.
task: Create aion_store_libsql::visibility module implementing VisibilityStore. Create the visibility table with indexed columns for standard fields and a JSON column for custom attributes. Implement record_visibility as an upsert, list_workflows as a parameterized query supporting all filter predicates, and count_workflows as the corresponding COUNT query. Use libSQL JSON functions for custom attribute filtering.

requirements.title: Visibility table schema with indexed standard fields
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL create a visibility table with columns: workflow_id (TEXT, PRIMARY KEY), run_id (TEXT), workflow_type (TEXT, indexed), status (TEXT, indexed), start_time (TEXT, indexed), close_time (TEXT, indexed), search_attributes (TEXT, JSON). Indexes SHALL exist on workflow_type, status, start_time, and close_time.
acceptance: The visibility table is created during store initialization.
Indexes exist on workflow_type, status, start_time, close_time.
The schema supports the full WorkflowSummary projection.

requirements.title: record_visibility upserts workflow summary
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement record_visibility as an INSERT OR REPLACE that upserts the full visibility record for a workflow, including current status, times, and search attributes.
acceptance: Recording a new workflow inserts a row.
Recording an existing workflow updates the row.
Search attributes are stored as valid JSON.
The upserted row is immediately queryable.

requirements.title: list_workflows with filter predicates
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement list_workflows to query the visibility table with WHERE clauses derived from ListWorkflowsFilter. Standard field filters use indexed column comparisons. Custom attribute filters use libSQL JSON extraction (json_extract). Results are ordered by start_time descending, with limit/offset pagination.
acceptance: Filtering by workflow_type returns only matching workflows.
Filtering by status returns only matching workflows.
Filtering by time range (started_after/before) returns workflows in range.
Filtering by custom attribute value returns matching workflows.
An empty filter returns all workflows (paginated).
Limit and offset pagination works correctly.

requirements.title: count_workflows
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement count_workflows as a COUNT query using the same filter logic as list_workflows.
acceptance: count_workflows with no filter returns the total workflow count.
count_workflows with a status filter returns the count of matching workflows.
count_workflows matches the length of list_workflows with the same filter.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement engine-level visibility updates — that is AV-004.
SHALL NOT implement server endpoints — that is AV-005.
SHALL NOT implement an Elasticsearch/Meilisearch backend — that is a future enhancement.
verification: Tests cover: upsert, filter by each standard field, filter by custom attribute, pagination, count.
